items.headline: Tom outscored every rep on call #1
items.detail: 7.5 overall - higher than Anthony (5.96 avg), Rye (5.17) & Bailey (3.37). His dentist metaphor for son buy-in landed a gold standard tag. One habit fix (fear bomb cycling) away from 8.5+. The guy who hasn't officially started is already the best closer on the roster.

items.headline: Rye conversation has natural timing before Monday
items.detail: 0 closes from 3 calls. E score 1.67. Jo Kelly prediction mismatch still open. Tom starting Monday reframes everything - Rye shifts from 'underperforming closer' to 'sales leader building the next rep'. The honours deferral chat in Bali lands perfectly here. Coaching role = growth, not demotion.

items.headline: $41.4K MRR is the real number now
items.detail: Down from ~$52K but that's zombie cleanup, not churn. 60 collecting families, 8 paused. CPBC at $26.94 (well under $35 warning). LTV:CAC = 75.8x. Stripe balance $35.3K. The base is honest now - growth from here counts.

items.headline: Exit interviews close the 97% silence gap
items.detail: 97% of cancellations leave zero feedback. Friday's WCI spec (auto email + SMS + 5-question form + 10min call booking) turns every churn into data. Cancellation form already live at /cancel-feedback/. This is a HubSpot workflow build - not a new product. Shell can be up Monday.

items.headline: Connection Assessment attacks the #1 churn driver
items.detail: 5 archetypes (Processor, Side-by-Side, Deflector, Night Owl, Volcano) give mentors a playbook before W1. 63% of churned families never got a mentor assigned. If archetypes feed into mentor matching at onboarding, you're solving activation failure at the source - not chasing it after the fact.

leo_actions: Refreshing MC data & pushing to GitHub
Gateway watchdog healthy - no stalls since recovery
Prepping Tom's Monday onboarding checklist (docs, contract, Schedule B)
Ready to build exit interview HubSpot workflow on your go
